tenders.title: Netherlands – Software-related services – Online cursussen Verdiepende Digitale basisvaardigheden

The tender does not involve physical digital infrastructure or kiosks, which are the company's primary offerings.
tenders.strategic_link: This opportunity does not directly serve PARTTEAM's strategic objectives related to smart cities or international expansion, as it does not involve digital infrastructure or public procurement in target markets.
tenders.recommended_action: No-bid; focus resources on tenders that align with core product offerings and strategic objectives.

tenders.title: Spain – Surveillance and security systems and devices – Suministro e instalación de cámaras de CCTV y su infraestructura en los Palacios de Justicia de la Comunidad Autónoma del País Vasco.

However, the sector is Smart Cities, which is a strategic focus area for the company. There might be potential to explore partnerships or integrations with existing products like digital signage for security monitoring purposes.
tenders.strategic_link: Objective 3 — Smart Cities and Public Procurement

private_opportunities.title: How much a mall wayfinding kiosk costs? - Fingoweb
private_opportunities.snippet:
1. Hardware volume discounts – Larger deployments can reduce unit pricing but increase upfront investment.
2. Infrastructure planning – More kiosks require careful coordination of power, network, and mounting points across the site.
3. Centralized content management – A higher number of devices demands robust backend systems for remote updates, monitoring, and analytics.
4. Maintenance and support – More units lead to increased servicing needs, SLA complexity, and spare parts logistics.

Wayfinding kiosk: Digital guide for mall visitors

Wayfinding kiosk: Digital guide for mall visitors

## Software customization and technical complexity
private_opportunities.bid_fit_summary: Fingoweb is exploring the costs associated with mall wayfinding kiosks, indicating a potential expansion or upgrade of their digital infrastructure. PARTTEAM could offer multimedia kiosks with centralized content management and remote monitoring capabilities, aligning with Fingoweb's needs for robust backend systems.
private_opportunities.strategic_link: Objective 3 — Smart Cities and Public Procurement
private_opportunities.recommended_action: Contact Fingoweb's procurement or project management team to discuss potential collaboration on their wayfinding kiosk deployment.

private_opportunities.title: Boost Engagement with Smart City Digital Kiosks Now
private_opportunities.snippet:
Real-time updates on public safety incidents
 Access to emergency services and contact information
 Location-based alerts on suspicious activity or extreme weather
 Emergency defibrillator access in case of cardiac arrest
 Identification scanning technology for better access control

#### City Wayfinding

Outdoor touch screen smart city digital kiosks are the perfect wayfinding tool for smart cities. Utilizing a touch screen smart city digital kiosk’s interactive maps, visitors and citizens of your city can easily locate points of interest, find their way around unfamiliar areas, and navigate public transportation. Smart city digital kiosks can be a great help for wayfinding with features like: [...] #### Civic Benefits

Smart city digital kiosks offer a wide range of benefits for cities aiming to improve civic services. For instance, they can improve communication between the city and its citizens by providing real-time information about city services, events, and promoting participation in government services like voting. Wayfinding functions help visitors and residents navigate their way through a city more efficiently. Smart city digital kiosks can also enable seamless payments for city services, such as parking and public transportation, reducing waiting times. Some other benefits include: [...] How do smart city digital kiosks boost civic engagement?

Smart city digital kiosks provide real-time city services, events, voting access, and wayfinding, fostering transparency and efficient resource use while enabling incident reporting.

Can smart city digital kiosks support local economies?

Yes, they feature advertising for businesses and tourism, increasing visibility, cutting costs, and offering promotions to drive customer access and revenue.

pretender_opportunities.title: [PDF] REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS 25-046-RFP PUBLIC SAFETY ...
pretender_opportunities.snippet: Award Process"): A. The Project Contact shall invite the Proposer with the highest score from the Proposal Evaluation Process to participate in contract negotiations with the City. B. Proposer and City shall negotiate the final terms and conditions of the Contract, pursuant to this RFP. The Contact shall contain the same, or substantially the same, terms and conditions as portrayed in this RFP, the Scope of Work, the Proposal, and the City's Standard Contract. C. Following negotiations, the City shall draft a contract for execution by all parties. If the Proposer fails to sign and return the Contact within seven (7) working days of its delivery to the Proposer, the City may determine, at its sole discretion, that the Proposer is nonresponsive to the terms of this RFP and initiate further [...] or by any other means. All proposals shall be submitted through the City’s e-Procurement Portal no later than: Friday, August 29, 2025 at 5:00 pm. A Proposer shall respond to the RFP and any exhibits, attachments, or amendments. A Proposer's failure to submit a Proposal in compliance with the procedures and timelines set forth in the RFP shall cause the Proposal to be deemed nonresponsive and rejected. A late Proposal shall not be accepted. Additional time to respond shall not be granted to any Proposer. 4.2. Questions and/or Objections It is to the City and Proposer's benefit that questions and objections are brought to the City's attention so it may issue necessary amendments to the RFP and help prevent the opening of defective proposals upon which the City could not award a Contract. [...] #25-046-RFP Title: Public Safety Technology Infrastructure Master Plan 22 B. Interviews shall be attended by the individual identified in the Proposal as the primary contact to the City on a day-to-day basis and members of the proposed team identified in the "Personnel" section of the Mandatory Qualifications and Experience. C. Following all interviews, the Evaluation Team will re-rank the Proposers to determine the highest ranked Proposers. Then, the Project Contact will open the sealed Cost Proposals for the highest ranked Proposers and re-rank the highest ranked Proposers based on overall ranking and Cost Proposal. 7.3. pretender_opportunities.title: [PDF] City of Los Angeles - SmartLA 2028
pretender_opportunities.snippet:
# and visitors, specifically focused on 

# technology in the rights-of-way used to 

# deliver services in the cityscape 

# (e.g. IoT, 5G, fiber, etc). 

Participating Departments: 

• Department of Water & Power 

• Information Technology Agency 

• Office of the Mayor 

• Los Angeles World Airports 

• Public Works Bureau of Sanitation 

• Public Works Bureau of Engineering 

• Public Works Bureau of Street Lighting 

• Department on Disability 11 SmartLA 2028 

# INFRASTRUCTURE 

Smart City Infrastructure is the 

fundamental, physical technology 

required to deliver connectivity, sensors, 

and communications across our urban 

environment. This infrastructure includes 

wireless and wireline communications, as 

well as strategic hardware assets. These [...] information sharing. 

Smart City Digital Services & Applications 

Applications, websites, and other services used to digitally deliver City services 

to the public. 

Smart City Connectivity & Digital Inclusion 

Addressing digital inclusion by integrating public and private offerings to help 

L.A. residents, L.A. businesses, and those with disabilities to get online and 

connected. 

Smart City Governance 

Coordination and investment of Smart City efforts across City departments. 

# COMPONENTS OF A DIGITAL AND 

# CONNECTED LOS ANGELES This page is intentionally left blank 10 SmartLA 2028 

# INFRASTRUCTURE 

# Physical technology used to deliver Smart 

# City technologies to residents, businesses, 

# and visitors, specifically focused on [...] • Public Works Bureau of Street Services 

• Department on Disability 27 SmartLA 2028 

# DIGITAL SERVICES 

Smart City Digital Services are the apps, websites, and other public facing technologies 

used by the public to access City information, request City services, or simply engage 

their government. Unlike Smart City Infrastructure or Data, Digital Services are highly 

visible and tangible for our residents, businesses, and visitors. In the beginning, residents 

had to visit a City of Los Angeles facility to get information or request a public service. 

With the invention of the telephone, they could simply call. Then, websites allowed online 

access from a computer. Smartphone apps made these capabilities available anytime, 

anywhere.

pretender_opportunities.strategic_link: Objective 3 — Smart Cities and Public Procurement
pretender_opportunities.recommended_action: Register with the City of Los Angeles procurement portal and prepare a product brief highlighting relevant smart city solutions.

executive_takeaway: The most significant opportunity this week is the Romanian tender for ticket-issuing machines, which aligns closely with our expertise in ticket vending solutions. Leadership should prioritize preparing a bid for this tender to strengthen our presence in the Romanian market. Additionally, the private opportunity with Shining Ltd offers a chance to expand our smart city digital kiosk offerings. Engaging with both the Romanian tender and Shining Ltd should be prioritized to maximize our strategic objectives in smart cities.
final_brief:
Business Opportunities Snapshot — Smart Cities (Last 7 Days)

── LIVE EU TENDERS ──────────────────────────────────
• Romania – Ticket-issuing machines — ROU | Deadline: Not specified | Value: Not specified | Urgency: Immediate
  Fit: This tender aligns well with PARTTEAM & OEMKIOSKS' capabilities, particularly with our TVM — Ticket Vending Machines, which are suitable for the e-ticketing system required in this project.
  Action: Proceed with preparing a bid proposal to leverage our expertise in ticket vending solutions and expand our presence in the Romanian market.
  Link: https://ted.europa.eu/en/notice/-/detail/134219-2026

• Spain – Surveillance and security systems and devices — ESP | Deadline: 2026-03-30 | Value: Not specified | Urgency: Monitor
  Fit: This tender involves the supply and installation of CCTV systems, which does not directly align with PARTTEAM & OEMKIOSKS' core product offerings.
  Action: Monitor the tender for potential partnership opportunities or future related tenders that align more closely with core products.
  Link: https://ted.europa.eu/en/notice/-/detail/134251-2026

• Netherlands – Software-related services — NLD | Deadline: 2026-04-13 | Value: Not specified | Urgency: Monitor
  Fit: This tender focuses on software-related services for online courses in digital skills, which does not align with PARTTEAM & OEMKIOSKS' core hardware and digital signage products.
  Action: No-bid; focus resources on tenders that align with core product offerings and strategic objectives.
  Link: https://ted.europa.eu/en/notice/-/detail/134229-2026

── PRIVATE OPPORTUNITIES ────────────────────────────
• Boost Engagement with Smart City Digital Kiosks Now | Shining Ltd | Published: Not specified
  Fit: A private company is expanding its smart city digital kiosk offerings, focusing on real-time updates, emergency services, and wayfinding.
  Action: Contact the company to discuss potential collaboration on smart city digital kiosk deployments.
  Link: https://www.shiningltd.com/smart-city-digital-kiosk/?srsltid=AfmBOooDvcX7r2ynoTEr8hFYWvLfk9pMLVhrvLBNNdCWJmDcobvBGdLj

• How much a mall wayfinding kiosk costs? - Fingoweb | Fingoweb | Published: Not specified

── PRE-TENDER SIGNALS ───────────────────────────────
• [PDF] REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS 25-046-RFP PUBLIC SAFETY ... | Government Navigator | Published: Not specified
  Signal: The public authority is planning a public safety project that likely involves digital infrastructure.
  Action: Register with the authority and prepare a product brief highlighting relevant smart city solutions.
  Link: https://media.governmentnavigator.com/media/bid/1753378590_Public_Safety_Technology_Infrastructure_Master_Plan.pdf

• [PDF] City of Los Angeles - SmartLA 2028 | City of Los Angeles | Published: Not specified
  Signal: The City of Los Angeles is planning a SmartLA 2028 initiative focusing on technology in public spaces.
  Action: Register with the City of Los Angeles procurement portal and prepare a product brief highlighting relevant smart city solutions.
  Link: https://ita.lacity.gov/sites/g/files/wph1626/files/2021-05/SmartLA2028%20-%20Smart%20City%20Strategy.pdf

• Contract Announcements Archives - Smart City | Smart City Blog | Published: Not specified
  Signal: The Greater Columbus Convention Center is planning to enhance its digital infrastructure as part of a smart city initiative.
  Action: Register with the Greater Columbus Convention Center and prepare a product brief highlighting relevant digital signage and kiosk solutions.
  Link: https://blog.smartcity.com/tag/contract-announcements/

── EXECUTIVE TAKEAWAY ───────────────────────────────
The most significant opportunity this week is the Romanian tender for ticket-issuing machines, which aligns closely with our expertise in ticket vending solutions. Leadership should prioritize preparing a bid for this tender to strengthen our presence in the Romanian market. Additionally, the private opportunity with Shining Ltd offers a chance to expand our smart city digital kiosk offerings. Engaging with both the Romanian tender and Shining Ltd should be prioritized to maximize our strategic objectives in smart cities.
